---
title: "GIS"
author: "Simon Gendrisch"
format: html
editor: source
---

```{r, packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(leaflet)
library(units)
library(ggspatial)
library(lubridate)
```


```{r, Load layers}
qfield <- st_read("./data/GIS/test/qfield_test_17.04.gpkg")
# QField points
qfield_wp <- st_read("./data/GIS/test/survey_data.gpkg", layer = "survey_data")

gaia <- st_read("./data/GIS/test/gaia_test_17.04.gpx", layer = "track_points")
gaia_line <- st_read("./data/GIS/test/gaia_test_17.04.gpx", layer = "tracks")

# Garmin track File
garmin <- st_read("./data/GIS/26_05_04pilot/Track_2026-05-04 125104.gpx", layer = "track_points")
garmin_line <- st_read("./data/GIS/26_05_04pilot/Track_2026-05-04 125104.gpx", layer = "tracks")

#Garmin POI File
garmin_wp <- st_read("./data/GIS/26_05_04pilot/Waypoints_04-MAY-26.gpx", layer = "waypoints")


plot(garmin_line)

bbox_2 <- st_bbox(gaia)

```

```{r, Define off and on effort times}

date <- as.Date(garmin$time[1])

effort_times <- data.frame(
  off_effort = as.POSIXct(paste(date, c("10:25:00"))) ,
  on_effort = as.POSIXct(paste(date, c("10:33:00")))
)

garmin <- garmin %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(effort = ifelse(
    time >= effort_times$off_effort & time <= effort_times$on_effort, 
    0,
    1
  )) %>% 
  ungroup()

garmin %>% 
  filter(effort == 0)

```


```{r, cleaning qfield_wp for visualisation}

wp_clean <- qfield_wp %>%
  filter(id >= 5)


garmin_wp_dis <- st_as_sf(garmin_wp,  crs = 4326)
st_distance(garmin_wp_dis[3,], garmin_wp_dis[4,])

```


```{r, Plot test 1}

bbox_1 <- st_bbox(garmin)

test_1 <- ggplot() +
  #geom_sf(data = qfield, aes(color = "QField"), linewidth = .8) +
  #geom_sf(data = gaia_line, aes(color = "Gaia GPS"), linewidth = .8) +
  geom_sf(data = garmin_line, aes(color = "Garmin"), linewidth = .8) +
  geom_sf(data = garmin_wp, aes(color = "Garmin Waypoints"), size = 5, alpha = .5) +
  #geom_sf(data = wp_clean, aes(color = "QField Waypoints"), size = 5, alpha = .5) +
  annotation_scale(location = "bl", width_hint = .5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("QField" = "blue", 
                                 "Gaia GPS" = "red", 
                                 "Garmin" = "green",
                                 "Garmin Waypoints" = "darkgreen",
                                 "QField Waypoints" = "darkblue")) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox_1["xmin"], bbox_1["xmax"]), ylim = c(bbox_1["ymin"], bbox_1["ymax"]+.0003)) +
  labs(title = "GPS Track Comparison Test Dolphin Boat", color = "Source") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  )

test_1

plot(garmin_wp)
```

```{r, Plot test 2}

test_2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = qfield, aes(color = "QField"), linewidth = .8) +
  geom_sf(data = gaia_line, aes(color = "Gaia GPS"), linewidth = .8) +
  geom_sf(data = garmin_line, aes(color = "Garmin"), linewidth = .8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("QField" = "blue", 
                                 "Gaia GPS" = "red")) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox_2["xmin"], bbox_2["xmax"]), ylim = c(bbox_2["ymin"], bbox_2["ymax"]+.0003)) +
  labs(title = "GPS Track Comparison Test 2", color = "Source") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  )

```

```{r, interactive plot}
leaflet() %>%
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%
  # addPolylines(data = st_transform(gaia_line, 4326),
#               color = "red", weight = 2, label = "Gaia GPS") %>%
  addPolylines(data = st_transform(garmin_line, 4326),
               color = "green", weight = 2, label = "Garmin") %>%
 # addPolylines(data = st_transform(qfield, 4326),
  #             color = "blue", weight = 2, label = "QField") %>% 
  
  addMarkers(data = st_transform(garmin_wp, 4326)) %>% 
  addScaleBar(position = "bottomleft") %>% 
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",        # topleft, topright, bottomleft, bottomright
    colors   = c("red", "green", "blue"),
    labels   = c("Gaia GPS", "Garmin", "QField"),
    title    = "GPS Source",
    opacity  = 1
  )
```

```{r, Compare distance between points}

q_wp <- st_as_sfc(wp_clean)

g_wp <- st_as_sfc(garmin_wp)

st_distance(q_wp, g_wp)

```





```{r, Export}

ggsave(filename = "GPS_test_db.png", test_1, width = 1920, height = 1080, dpi = 200, units = "px")
ggsave(filename = "GPS_test_2.png", test_2, width = 1920, height = 1080, dpi = 200, units = "px")

```


```{r, Heatmap test}
# =============================================================================
# Simulated Dolphin Distribution Heatmap — Proposal Figure
# Study area: Strait of Gibraltar (GTW strata)
# Note: Simulated data only — for illustrative purposes
# =============================================================================

# ── 1. Packages ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# install.packages(c("sf", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "MASS", "metR", "ggspatial",
#                    "viridis", "scales", "ggfx"))

library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)        # kde2d
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(ggspatial)   # scale bar + north arrow

# ── 2. Load study area ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

area <- st_read("data/GTW_split_acc.gpkg", quiet = TRUE)

# Fix any invalid geometries (duplicate vertices, degenerate edges, etc.)
sf_use_s2(FALSE)          # switch to GEOS engine — avoids s2 strict validation
area <- st_make_valid(area)

# Reproject to WGS84 for plotting with lat/lon axes
area_wgs <- st_transform(area, crs = 4326)
area_wgs <- st_make_valid(area_wgs)   # re-validate after reprojection

# Bounding box
bb <- st_bbox(area_wgs)
xmin <- as.numeric(bb["xmin"]); xmax <- as.numeric(bb["xmax"])
ymin <- as.numeric(bb["ymin"]); ymax <- as.numeric(bb["ymax"])

# ── 3. Simulate dolphin sightings ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar concentrate near the shelf edge (South
# stratum) and along the main shipping lane corridor.  We place three
# overlapping Gaussian clusters to mimic this pattern.

set.seed(42)

# Helper: draw n points from a bivariate normal, keep only those inside area
sim_points <- function(n_target, mu_x, mu_y, sd_x, sd_y, area_sf,
                       max_attempts = 50000) {
  pts <- data.frame(
    x = rnorm(max_attempts, mu_x, sd_x),
    y = rnorm(max_attempts, mu_y, sd_y)
  )
  pts_sf <- st_as_sf(pts, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)
  inside  <- st_within(pts_sf, st_union(area_sf), sparse = FALSE)[, 1]
  pts[inside, ][seq_len(min(n_target, sum(inside))), ]
}

area_union <- st_union(area_wgs)

# Cluster 1 — shelf-edge / South stratum (highest density)
cl1 <- sim_points(180, mu_x = -5.330, mu_y = 36.080,
                  sd_x = 0.025,  sd_y = 0.018, area_sf = area_wgs)

# Cluster 2 — central corridor (moderate density)
cl2 <- sim_points(120, mu_x = -5.360, mu_y = 36.105,
                  sd_x = 0.030,  sd_y = 0.020, area_sf = area_wgs)

# Cluster 3 — eastern approach (lower density)
cl3 <- sim_points( 70, mu_x = -5.295, mu_y = 36.110,
                  sd_x = 0.020,  sd_y = 0.015, area_sf = area_wgs)

sightings <- bind_rows(cl1, cl2, cl3)

# ── 4. Kernel density estimation ──────────────────────────────────────────────

h_x <- 0.018   # bandwidth (degrees) — tune for visual smoothness
h_y <- 0.014

kde <- kde2d(
  x = sightings$x, y = sightings$y,
  h = c(h_x, h_y),
  n = 300,
  lims = c(xmin - 0.01, xmax + 0.01, ymin - 0.01, ymax + 0.01)
)

# Convert KDE output to a data frame
kde_df <- expand.grid(x = kde$x, y = kde$y) |>
  mutate(density = as.vector(kde$z))

# Mask: only keep cells whose centre falls inside the study area
kde_sf <- st_as_sf(kde_df, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)
in_area <- st_within(kde_sf, area_union, sparse = FALSE)[, 1]
kde_df$density[!in_area] <- NA

# Rescale density to 0–1 for intuitive legend
kde_df <- kde_df |>
  mutate(density_scaled = rescale(density, to = c(0, 1)))

# ── 5. Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

stratum_colors <- c("South" = "#212842",
    "East" = "#4E5B9C",
    "West" = "#5C9EAD")

p <- ggplot() +

  # KDE heatmap (raster tiles)
  geom_raster(
    data    = filter(kde_df, !is.na(density)),
    mapping = aes(x = x, y = y, fill = density_scaled),
    interpolate = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    option  = "inferno",
    name    = "Relative\ndensity",
    na.value = "transparent",
    limits  = c(0, 1),
    breaks  = c(0, 0.5, 1),
    labels  = c("Low", "Medium", "High")
  ) +

  # Study area boundary (strata outlines)
  geom_sf(
    data  = area_wgs,
    aes(colour = name),
    fill  = "transparent",
    linewidth = 0.7,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = stratum_colors,
    name   = "Stratum"
  ) +

  # Simulated sighting locations (subtle)
  geom_point(
    data  = sightings,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    shape = 21, size = 1.4,
    fill  = "white", colour = "grey30", alpha = 0.55, stroke = 0.3
  ) +

  # Scale bar + north arrow (requires ggspatial)
  annotation_scale(
    location = "bl", width_hint = 0.25,
    text_col = "black", line_col = "black", bar_cols = c("white", "black")
  ) +
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "br",
    which_north = "true",
    style = north_arrow_minimal(text_col = "black", line_col = "black",
                                fill = "black")
  ) +

  # Labels
  labs(
    title    = "Simulated Common Dolphin Distribution",
    subtitle = "Strait of Gibraltar study area  |  Illustrative data only",
    x        = "Longitude",
    y        = "Latitude",
    caption  = "Note: Sighting locations are simulated for proposal purposes only."
  ) +

  # Coordinate system — lock aspect ratio
  coord_sf(
    xlim   = c(xmin - 0.005, xmax + 0.005),
    ylim   = c(ymin - 0.005, ymax + 0.005),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +

  # Theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
  ) +

  # Stack the two legends vertically
  guides(
    fill   = guide_colorbar(order = 1, barheight = 6, barwidth = 0.8,
                             ticks.colour = "white", frame.colour = "white"),
    colour = guide_legend(order = 2,
                          override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5))
  )

p
# ── 6. Save ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ggsave(
#   filename = "dolphin_heatmap.png",
#   plot     = p,
#   width    = 10,
#   height   = 8,
#   dpi      = 250,
#   bg       = "#0d1b2a"
# )

ggsave(filename = "relative_density.png", p, width = 1920, height = 1080, dpi = 200, units = "px")
message("✓ Saved: dolphin_heatmap.png")

```
